candidates.name: Sarah Huckabee Sanders
candidates.notes: Governor since 2023. First woman governor of Arkansas. Daughter of former Gov. Mike Huckabee (Southern Baptist pastor). Former White House Press Secretary under Trump. Ouachita Baptist University graduate. Signed LEARNS Act (school choice). Signed near-total abortion ban trigger law enforcement. Signed constitutional carry protection. Signed anti-CRT and anti-trans-in-schools legislation. Signed law allowing Ten Commandments in public buildings. Strong evangelical conservative.

profile.background: Former White House Press Secretary under President Trump; political consultant and campaign adviser; became Arkansas's first female governor and 47th governor in 2023.

candidates.name: John Boozman
candidates.notes: Senator since 2011. Heritage Action 71% current, 66% lifetime — solid but not top-tier conservative. NRA A rated. Former optometrist. Former House member (2001-2011). Pro-life. Supports border security. Some bipartisan compromise tendencies on appropriations and agriculture. Baptist. Ranking member of Senate Agriculture Committee. Lower Heritage score reflects occasional moderate votes on spending bills.

profile.education: University of Arkansas (attended), Southern College of Optometry (OD)
profile.background: Optometrist who co-founded a private practice. Former U.S. House member (2001-2011). Played football at University of Arkansas. Served on Rodgers school board.
profile.office: 555 Dirksen Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Tom Cotton
candidates.notes: Senator since 2015. Heritage Action 73% current, 77% lifetime. NRA A+ rated. Army veteran (Iraq and Afghanistan, Bronze Star). Harvard Law graduate. Hawkish on national security and immigration. Wrote NYT op-ed calling for military response to 2020 riots. Pro-life. Opposes all amnesty. Supports border wall. Strong China hawk. United Methodist (First United Methodist Church of Dardanelle). Potential 2028 presidential candidate.

profile.education: Harvard University (AB Government), Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Army veteran who served in Iraq and Afghanistan, earning a Bronze Star. Former attorney at Gibson Dunn. Served one term in U.S. House before winning Senate seat in 2014.
profile.office: 326 Russell Senate Office Building

profile.education: Harvard University (AB Economics), Georgetown University Law Center (JD/MSFS)
profile.background: Former Alaska Attorney General and Commissioner of Natural Resources. Marine Corps colonel with combat service in Afghanistan. Former White House aide and State Department official.
